title: Lightweight web contribution flow with built-in consent capture
context: The primary data-collection path in M0-M3 is curator-led (maintainer or reviewer collects and processes recordings). A lightweight web contribution flow would allow community members to submit recordings directly, but only under a partner consent-intake protocol — the project must not collect speaker PII without a partner organisation with an appropriate data-handling agreement. This is a backlog item, explicitly noted as 'Only with a partner consent-intake protocol' in TASKS.md. Any voice data collected must be ephemeral on the server side until it passes the CI gates; no recordings are stored without a complete ConsentRecord.
objective: Implement a lightweight web contribution form that guides contributors through recording a place-name pronunciation and capturing a machine-readable ConsentRecord, operating only under a partner consent-intake protocol and without storing speaker PII in the public corpus.
acceptanceCriteria: The contribution flow is only activated once a partner consent-intake protocol is in place and documented.
The ConsentRecord is captured as part of the contribution flow, covering: license grant, explicit voice-recording release, withdrawal terms, and CARE flag if applicable.
No speaker PII (legal name, contact, precise location) is stored in the public corpus; pseudonymous speaker ref only.
Submitted audio is held ephemerally until it passes the CI consent-completeness and license-allowlist gates; rejected submissions are discarded.
Guardian-consent path is implemented for potential minor/vulnerable-speaker submissions.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass.
Privacy/consent reviewer signs off the consent-capture UX before launch.
Maintainer reviews and merges the PR.
output: Web contribution flow (TypeScript/ESM) committed via PR with built-in ConsentRecord capture, operating under a partner consent-intake protocol, with privacy/consent reviewer sign-off.
